notes: Asserts the no-flicker streaming invariant for gemini-cli: ready→working→ready with the band staying solidly working across the streaming window and no premature ready before the turn-end signal. UNLIKE claudecode (whose transcript appends one assistant line per chunk, guarded by resolveAssistantStreaming, issue #102), Gemini's ChatRecordingService writes the assistant text to the JSONL exactly ONCE per turn (no intermediate partial-content disk records); only metadata-only $set/message_update records land mid-turn. So the streaming window produces no non-empty-content record for the parser's text-only/no-tool turn_done heuristic (parser.go ~L209) to misfire on, and the daemon emits exactly one working→ready at turn end. Recorded with the INTERACTIVE gemini-cli driver (gemini -y under tmux: one send + wait_turn + 6s trailing sleep), NOT headless `gemini -p` (a one-shot run exits before the daemon observes the terminal ready edge). turn_end anchors to streaming_window (not session_birth) so the matcher picks the post-turn ready, not the birth ready. PROVISIONAL on emission: daemon=full here depends on Gemini's single-write recording; record must live-confirm zero working→ready transitions across the streaming window before the terminal record on a genuinely long prose turn. observations.model ('gemini-3-flash-preview') is the live-verified default boot model and is provisional — a different live default is a string update, not a daemon bug.

text: Session appears in ready shortly after launch
Launcher PID bound by cwd shortly after launch
User prompt for a multi-sentence response drives ready → working

During streaming the session stays solidly in working. Gemini persists no intermediate partial-content record (only metadata $set/message_update); none must promote to ready before the terminal text-only assistant record.
The single final text-only 'gemini' record (complete prose, no tool calls) lands; working → ready
